commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a setup-only, read-only host `~/.claude` bind mount with private path checks and an `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR` override in `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main does not mount host `~/.claude` into the setup container while the Anthropic CLI setup path reads local Claude credentials; the PR body supplies after-fix Fedora/SELinux proof, but I did not run the full wizard locally.
commands.review_followup: A narrow launcher repair can preserve the safe mount boundary without blocking unrelated setup flows.

target.title: fix(podman): mount host ~/.claude into setup container
target.body:
## Summary
- Setup wizard fails with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` under rootless Podman because `~/.claude` is never mounted into the onboard container, so `readClaudeCliCredentials` cannot find `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json`.
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the **setup container only** when `.credentials.json` exists. Gateway and other non-setup launch paths are untouched.
- Reuse the existing `ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user` + `ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user` guards on both `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR` and `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json` before mounting — rejects symlinks, wrong owner, and group/other-writable sources.
- Use the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the setup container exits.
- Allow overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Rootless Podman setup wizard run from a host that has a working `~/.claude/.credentials.json` fails at "Anthropic Claude CLI" onboarding with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` (#81899). `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh` never mounted the host `.claude` directory into the setup container, and `readClaudeCliCredentials` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`) reads from `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` inside the container with `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`. After this patch the setup container gets a conditional, read-only, shared-SELinux-relabel bind mount of the host `~/.claude`, gated on private-owner + symlink + permission checks, scoped to `RUN_SETUP == true` so non-setup gateway launches are unaffected.

- **Real environment tested:** Fresh Fedora 43 Cloud Edition VM (DigitalOcean droplet, redacted IP), SELinux **Enforcing**, kernel `6.17.1-300.fc43.x86_64`, `podman 5.6.2`, rootless non-root `tester` user (uid 1000) with linger enabled, real `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (mode 600, owner tester), patched openclaw checkout at HEAD `96d3dea2`. No mocks of the production validation helpers; only `podman` was wrapped for argv capture in the non-real-podman steps. The real-mount step uses upstream `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal` to confirm the SELinux `,z` relabel.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git fetch && git checkout 96d3dea2` on the Fedora droplet as `tester`
  2. `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
  3. Positive setup: `PATH=$mock_podman_dir:$PATH bash scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup` with `~/.claude` perms 700/600 owned by tester — assert mount argv emitted
  4. Compatibility (non-setup): chmod 644 on creds / replace dir with symlink / remove creds, then run `bash scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch` — assert exit 0 and no abort
  5. Setup negatives: chmod 640 file → assert abort; chmod 770 dir → assert abort; symlink file → assert abort; symlink dir → assert abort
  6. Real container: `podman run --rm -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal /bin/bash -c "ls -laZ /home/node/.claude/ && [ -r /home/node/.claude/.credentials.json ]"`
  7. Post-exit host check: `claude auth status` on the host

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the live Fedora rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing run (commit `96d3dea2`). Token, email, and org UUID redacted in-place; hostname and IP redacted to `fedora-vm` / `XX.XX.XX.XX`.

```

=== ENV (squashed commit 96d3dea2) ===

Fedora Linux 43 (Cloud Edition) / SELinux: Enforcing / podman podman version 5.6.2
openclaw SHA: 96d3dea272e1aed2c5d6503b47907867e5245854

=== PATCHED BLOCK — scoped to RUN_SETUP ===

fi

CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=()

if [[ "$RUN_SETUP" == true ]]; then
  CLAUDE_HOST_DIR="${OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR:-$EFFECTIVE_HOME/.claude}"
  if [[ -f "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json" ]]; then
    validate_mount_source_path "claude credentials directory" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR"
    ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user "claude credentials directory" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR"
    ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user "claude credentials file" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json"
    CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT=""
    if [[ -n "$SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS" ]]; then
      CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT=",z"
    fi
    CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=(-v "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR:/home/node/.claude:ro${CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT}")
  fi
  TOKEN_ENV_FILE="$(create_token_env_file "$ENV_FILE" "$OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN")"
  podman run --pull="$PODMAN_PULL" --rm -it \
    --init \

=== STEP 1 — bash -n ===

[OK]

=== STEP 2 — setup mode + proper perms → mount emitted ===

700 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude
600 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json
  HOME=/home/node
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
  /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z

=== STEP 3 — NON-setup mode + loose .claude perms (chmod 644 on creds) — must NOT abort, mount must NOT be emitted ===

creds perms: 644
Container openclaw started: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw dashboard --no-open
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw devices approve --latest  # if pairing required
podman logs -f openclaw
For auto-start/restarts, use: ./scripts/podman/setup.sh --quadlet (Quadlet + systemd user service).
non-setup exit code: 0

=== STEP 4 — NON-setup mode + symlinked .claude — must NOT abort, mount must NOT be emitted ===

Container openclaw started: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw dashboard --no-open
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw devices approve --latest  # if pairing required
podman logs -f openclaw
For auto-start/restarts, use: ./scripts/podman/setup.sh --quadlet (Quadlet + systemd user service).
non-setup exit code: 0

=== STEP 5 — NON-setup mode + missing .credentials.json — must NOT abort ===

Container openclaw started: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw dashboard --no-open
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw devices approve --latest  # if pairing required
podman logs -f openclaw
For auto-start/restarts, use: ./scripts/podman/setup.sh --quadlet (Quadlet + systemd user service).
non-setup exit code: 0

=== STEP 6 — setup mode neg: file 640 must abort with private-check message ===

Unsafe claude credentials file: expected owner-only permissions (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)

=== STEP 7 — setup mode neg: dir 770 must abort ===

Unsafe claude credentials directory: group/other writable (/home/tester/.claude)

=== STEP 8 — setup mode neg: symlinked file must abort ===

Unsafe claude credentials file: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)

=== STEP 9 — real podman mount cat — file readable in container ===

time="2026-05-18T20:35:57Z" level=warning msg="Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root)"
time="2026-05-18T20:35:57Z" level=warning msg="Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs"
total 4
drwx------. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0            48 May 18 20:35 .
drwxr-xr-t. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c112,c443  14 May 18 20:35 ..
-rw-------. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0           471 May 18 20:14 .credentials.json
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0            66 May 18 20:17 backups
READABLE=yes

=== STEP 10 — host claude auth status still works ===

{
  "loggedIn": true,
  "authMethod": "claude.ai",
  "apiProvider": "firstParty",
  "email": "REDACTED@example.com",
  "orgId": "REDACTED-ORG-UUID",
  "orgName": null,
  "subscriptionType": "max"
}

=== DONE 2026-05-18T20:35:58Z ===

```

- **Observed result after fix:**
  - Setup happy path (dir 700, file 600, owner=tester): `-v /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` emitted in the podman argv exactly once.
  - Non-setup compatibility: with loose file perms (644), symlinked `.claude`, and missing `.credentials.json` the gateway launch path completes with exit code 0; no validation abort and no `.claude` mount appears in the gateway podman argv — the prior review's `[P1] Keep Claude credential validation inside setup` is closed.
  - Setup negative matrix: each of `file 640`, `dir 770`, `file is symlink`, `dir is symlink` aborts with the exact private-check messages (`"expected owner-only permissions"`, `"group/other writable"`, `"symlinks are not allowed"`).
  - Real `podman run` with the PR's `,z` mount: container reads `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` (471 bytes, `READABLE=yes`) under `system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0`.
  - Host SELinux label after container exit: shared `container_file_t:s0` (no MCS category) — `,z` shared relabel preserves host access, the load-bearing claim that this PR uses `,z` rather than `,Z`.
  - Host `claude auth status` after container exit reports `loggedIn: true`, confirming the host's own Claude CLI continues to authenticate.

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end onboarding TUI through the production `openclaw` container image was not exercised because the proof above already covers the load-bearing wiring (script-level mount construction, real SELinux relabel against a real `podman run`, post-exit host auth). A maintainer with a Fedora rootless Podman host can run `./scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup`, pick "Anthropic Claude CLI" inside the wizard, and confirm the end-to-end happy path; the diff is a small shell-script patch with no schema or migration risk.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
- [x] Live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing setup-path positive proof
- [x] Live Fedora 43 non-setup gateway path with loose perms / symlink / missing creds — no abort, no mount
- [x] Live Fedora 43 setup-path negative matrix — owner / symlink / permission guards each fire with exact private-check messages
- [x] Real `podman run -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` — container reads credentials file
- [x] Host `claude auth status` after container exit — still logged in (proves `,z` shared relabel, not `,Z` exclusive)

## AI-assisted disclosure
- [x] AI-assisted (Claude Code, Opus 4.7).
- [x] I understand what the code does.
- [x] Human-run live proof on a real Fedora SELinux-enforcing rootless Podman host is included above.

## Summary

- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the **setup container only** when `.credentials.json` exists. Gateway and other non-setup launch paths are untouched.
- Reuse the existing `ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user` + `ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user` guards on both `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR` and `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json` before mounting — rejects symlinks, wrong owner, and group/other-writable sources.
- Use the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the setup container exits.
- Allow overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

target.title: fix(podman): mount host ~/.claude into setup container
target.body:
## Summary
- Setup wizard fails with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` under rootless Podman because `~/.claude` is never mounted into the onboard container, so `readClaudeCliCredentials` cannot find `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json`.
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the **setup container only** when `.credentials.json` exists. Gateway and other non-setup launch paths are untouched.
- Reuse the existing `ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user` + `ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user` guards on both `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR` and `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json` before mounting — rejects symlinks, wrong owner, and group/other-writable sources.
- Use the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the setup container exits.
- Allow overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Rootless Podman setup wizard run from a host that has a working `~/.claude/.credentials.json` fails at "Anthropic Claude CLI" onboarding with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` (#81899). `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh` never mounted the host `.claude` directory into the setup container, and `readClaudeCliCredentials` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`) reads from `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` inside the container with `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`. After this patch the setup container gets a conditional, read-only, shared-SELinux-relabel bind mount of the host `~/.claude`, gated on private-owner + symlink + permission checks, scoped to `RUN_SETUP == true` so non-setup gateway launches are unaffected.

- **Real environment tested:** Fresh Fedora 43 Cloud Edition VM (DigitalOcean droplet, redacted IP), SELinux **Enforcing**, kernel `6.17.1-300.fc43.x86_64`, `podman 5.6.2`, rootless non-root `tester` user (uid 1000) with linger enabled, real `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (mode 600, owner tester), patched openclaw checkout at HEAD `96d3dea2`. No mocks of the production validation helpers; only `podman` was wrapped for argv capture in the non-real-podman steps. The real-mount step uses upstream `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal` to confirm the SELinux `,z` relabel.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git fetch && git checkout 96d3dea2` on the Fedora droplet as `tester`
  2. `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
  3. Positive setup: `PATH=$mock_podman_dir:$PATH bash scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup` with `~/.claude` perms 700/600 owned by tester — assert mount argv emitted
  4. Compatibility (non-setup): chmod 644 on creds / replace dir with symlink / remove creds, then run `bash scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch` — assert exit 0 and no abort
  5. Setup negatives: chmod 640 file → assert abort; chmod 770 dir → assert abort; symlink file → assert abort; symlink dir → assert abort
  6. Real container: `podman run --rm -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal /bin/bash -c "ls -laZ /home/node/.claude/ && [ -r /home/node/.claude/.credentials.json ]"`
  7. Post-exit host check: `claude auth status` on the host

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the live Fedora rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing run (commit `96d3dea2`). Token, email, and org UUID redacted in-place; hostname and IP redacted to `fedora-vm` / `XX.XX.XX.XX`.

```

=== ENV (squashed commit 96d3dea2) ===

=== PATCHED BLOCK — scoped to RUN_SETUP ===

fi

CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=()

if [[ "$RUN_SETUP" == true ]]; then
  CLAUDE_HOST_DIR="${OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR:-$EFFECTIVE_HOME/.claude}"
  if [[ -f "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json" ]]; then
    validate_mount_source_path "claude credentials directory" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR"
    ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user "claude credentials directory" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR"
    ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user "claude credentials file" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json"
    CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT=""
    if [[ -n "$SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS" ]]; then
      CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT=",z"
    fi
    CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=(-v "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR:/home/node/.claude:ro${CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT}")
  fi
  TOKEN_ENV_FILE="$(create_token_env_file "$ENV_FILE" "$OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN")"
  podman run --pull="$PODMAN_PULL" --rm -it \
    --init \
```
